text:
To determine how many times Sandra has to babysit, we first need to calculate the total number of eggs she needs for making 15 Spanish flans.

Since each Spanish flan requires 3 eggs, the total number of eggs needed is:
\[ 15 \text{ flans} \times 3 \text{ eggs/flan} = 45 \text{ eggs} \]

Sandra's neighbor gives her a basket of 9 eggs every time she babysits. To find out how many times she needs to babysit, we divide the total number of eggs needed by the number of eggs she receives each time she babysits:
\[ \frac{45 \text{ eggs}}{9 \text{ eggs/babysitting}} = 5 \text{ babysittings} \]

So, Sandra has to babysit 5 times.
